extractions.problem: Identifying a reliable miRNA signature for diagnosis and prognosis of renal clear cell carcinoma (KIRC), as individual miRNA markers suffer from limited reproducibility across studies.
extractions.method: Comprehensive analysis of miRNA expression profiles from TCGA (516 KIRC patients) using limma differential expression, ROC curve analysis, Kaplan-Meier survival analysis, and univariate/multivariate Cox regression.
extractions.finding: A three-miRNA signature was identified that effectively diagnoses KIRC with high sensitivity/specificity and independently predicts overall survival, classifying patients into high- and low-risk groups with significantly different outcomes.
extractions.metric: sensitivity, specificity, AUC, Kaplan-Meier OS/DFS, Cox regression HR
extractions.result: sensitivity=98.3%, specificity=97.2%, 63 DE miRNAs (34 up, 29 down), independent prognostic factor for OS

extractions.problem: Improving human embryonic stem cell differentiation into definitive endoderm (DE) remains challenging and costly due to reliance on expensive growth factors like activin A.
extractions.method: Three orthogonal drug repurposing approaches (ssCMAP/LINCS, correlation-based, pathway/TF-based) to identify candidate DE inducers, validated using a SOX17-mNeonGreen CRISPR reporter hESC line with FACS and RNA-seq.
extractions.finding: Identified BRD-K42644990 as a novel compound that induces DE formation without activin A, likely via MYC pathway inhibition, achieving equivalent differentiation efficiency with 90% cost reduction when added to the standard protocol.
extractions.dataset: H1 hESC SOX17-mNG reporter line, GEO: GSE75748 (H1), GSE109658 (H9)
extractions.metric: % CXCR4+ cells, % SOX17-mNG+ cells (FACS), DE-induction efficiency
extractions.baseline: standard AA+CHIR protocol (100 ng/mL activin A + 3 µM CHIR)
extractions.result: 90% cost reduction with equivalent efficiency, 999 candidate inducers identified, 11/14 compounds induced DE

extractions.problem: The molecular mechanism by which citral suppresses human prostate cancer is unknown, and identifying drug targets that kill metastatic prostate cancer cells is critically needed.
extractions.method: Citral isolated from lemongrass oil via MPLC/HPLC, evaluated by MTT assay, flow cytometry, DNA fragmentation, Hoechst/PI staining, qPCR, Western blotting, molecular docking, and in silico ADMET/PASS prediction.
extractions.finding: Citral suppresses prostate cancer cell lipogenesis through AMPK phosphorylation activation and downregulation of FASN, ACC, HMGR, and SREBP1, and induces apoptosis by upregulating BAX and downregulating Bcl-2.
extractions.dataset: PC-3 and PC3M prostate cancer cell lines
extractions.metric: IC50, cell viability (MTT), apoptosis rate, protein expression levels
extractions.result: ADMET predicted safe drug-like properties, Pa>0.7 for apoptosis-related activities

extractions.problem: Adequate protective vaccines against Klebsiella pneumoniae are lacking, and identifying B cell epitopes of virulence-associated fimbriae antigens is needed for rational vaccine design.
extractions.method: Immunoinformatics approach using IEDB (7 tools), ABCpred, and conformational epitope prediction (DiscoTope, ElliPro) on type 1 fimbriae antigens (FimA, FimF, FimG, FimH), followed by antigenicity, toxicity, allergenicity, and human similarity screening.
extractions.finding: Four final B cell epitopes (one per Fim protein) were identified that are immunogenic, antigenic, non-toxic, non-allergenic, and dissimilar to human peptides, with suitable physicochemical properties for vaccine candidates.
extractions.dataset: K. pneumoniae type 1 fimbriae proteins FimA (CDO12578.1), FimF (CDO12574.1), FimG (CDO12573.1), FimH (CDO12572.1)
extractions.metric: epitope prediction scores, antigenicity, allergenicity, toxicity, human similarity
extractions.result: 4 final epitopes selected from 5+3+5+4 linear epitopes across four Fim proteins

extractions.problem: Developing more efficient isatin-based antitumor candidates by optimizing N-alkylindole-isatin conjugates for improved growth inhibitory profiles against cancer cells.
extractions.method: Synthesis of N-alkylindole-isatin conjugates, SRB cytotoxicity assay, NCI-60 panel screening, flow cytometric cell cycle and Annexin V-FITC/PI apoptosis assays, CDK2 and Bcl-2 inhibition assays, and molecular docking.
extractions.finding: N-propylindole-5-methylisatin hybrid 8a displayed broad-spectrum antiproliferative activity, induced cell cycle arrest and apoptosis in T-47D breast cancer cells, and potently inhibited CDK2 and Bcl-2, with N-propyl group contributing significant hydrophobic interactions.
extractions.dataset: A-549, MDA-MB-231, HCT-116, NCI-60 panel, T-47D breast cancer cells
extractions.metric: IC50, GI50 (MG-MID), cell cycle distribution, apoptosis %

extractions.problem: Discovering anti-pancreatic cancer agents is complex and expensive; computational approaches are needed to predict molecules that simultaneously inhibit multiple PANC cell lines and PANC-related proteins.
extractions.method: Perturbation theory combined with multilayer perceptron machine learning (PTML-MLP) trained on ChEMBL protein inhibition data and GDSC cell line sensitivity data, using molecular descriptors to predict multi-target inhibitors.
extractions.finding: Two PTML-MLP models with >78% accuracy were built and used to design three new molecules predicted as versatile anti-PANC agents capable of simultaneously inhibiting caspase-1, TNF-alpha, IGF1R, and multiple PANC cell lines.
extractions.dataset: 3,833 chemicals, 9,705 cases from ChEMBL and GDSC v7.0, 34 biomolecular/cellular targets, 12 PANC cell lines
extractions.metric: accuracy, IC50 cutoffs for activity classification
extractions.result: accuracy >78%, 3 new molecules designed as multi-target anti-PANC candidates

extractions.problem: Predicting cell-penetrating anticancer peptides (Cp-ACPs) is challenging as no existing model simultaneously evaluates both anticancer and cell-penetrating properties of peptide sequences.
extractions.method: Parallel two-layer ML framework using RF, SVM, and XGBoost classifiers with hybrid compositional/physicochemical features and multilayered recursive feature elimination (RFE), fused via consensus hard-fusion strategy.
extractions.finding: CpACpP achieved high prediction accuracy for both ACP and CPP subclassifiers, and the consensus fusion method effectively predicted Cp-ACPs, outperforming existing tools ACPred, mACPpred, MLCPP, and CPPred-RF.
extractions.dataset: experimentally validated ACP and CPP datasets, independent Cp-ACP validation set
extractions.result: ACP ACC=0.98, AUC≈0.98; CPP ACC≈0.94, AUC≈0.95; Cp-ACP fusion AUC=0.94, SVM MCC=0.68

extractions.problem: It is largely unknown whether long non-coding RNAs (lncRNAs) are deregulated in SARS-CoV-2 infection and what transcription factors might regulate them during COVID-19.
extractions.method: In silico analysis of RNA-seq data (GSE147507) using edgeR differential expression, NPInter v4.0 for lncRNA interaction partners, KEGG/GO pathway analysis, and ChIPBase v2.0 for transcription factor binding at lncRNA promoters.
extractions.finding: 20 lncRNAs (including MALAT1, NEAT1) were upregulated and 4 downregulated in SARS-CoV-2 infected cells; these interact with >2800 genes and 422 miRNAs involved in viral infection and immune pathways, regulated by IRF1, IRF4, STAT1, and STAT3.
extractions.dataset: GSE147507 (A549, Calu-3 cell lines infected with SARS-CoV-2, COVID-19 patient lung tissue)
extractions.result: 20 upregulated + 4 downregulated lncRNAs, EGOT increased 3.0-4.6 fold, 2788 interacting genes/proteins

extractions.problem: Physapubenolide (PB) shows anticancer cytotoxicity but its molecular targets and mechanisms in melanoma are unknown, limiting clinical development.
extractions.method: In silico target prediction combining chemical similarity searching, QSAR, and molecular docking, validated with CCK8 viability assay, EdU proliferation, colony formation, Annexin V/PI apoptosis, Western blotting, and melanoma xenograft model.
extractions.finding: PB targets HMGCR, inhibiting the HMGCR/YAP pathway to suppress melanoma proliferation and migration, induce apoptosis, and restore vemurafenib sensitivity in resistant A-375 cells.
extractions.dataset: A-375, SK-MEL-5 melanoma cells, vemurafenib-resistant A-375, HaCaT normal cells, melanoma xenograft model
extractions.metric: IC50, cell viability, EdU-positive cells, apoptosis rate, protein expression
extractions.baseline: vemurafenib-sensitive A-375 cells, HaCaT normal keratinocytes
extractions.result: IC50=1.225 µM (A-375), 1.416 µM (SK-MEL-5) at 72h, no significant cytotoxicity in HaCaT at low concentrations

extractions.problem: Multiple in silico hSC-CM models exist but their drug response predictions have not been systematically compared against each other and against in vitro data.
extractions.method: Simulated 15 drug actions using simple pore-block model with experimental IC50 data for seven ion channels on three hSC-CM models (Koivumäki2018, Kernik2019, Paci2020), with sensitivity analysis of INa, ICaL, and IKr blockade.
extractions.finding: The three models show qualitatively consistent CTD90 changes but smaller magnitudes than in vitro; they differ in susceptibility to pro-arrhythmic events and automaticity suppression, reflecting variability in underlying experimental calibration data.
extractions.dataset: 15 drugs tested on 3 in silico hSC-CM models (Koivumäki2018, Kernik2019, Paci2020), in vitro hSC-CM data
extractions.metric: CTD90 (% change), APD90, arrhythmic event occurrence, MDP
extractions.result: sparfloxacin 10µM: +42% CTD90 in vitro vs +17%/+6%/+9% in silico; qualitative agreement but quantitative underestimation
